items.preprocessing: 6/7 - Implements adaptive downsampling via compute_downsample_factor() and downsample() (AREA interpolation, suitable for huge 30k images), Gaussian blur with odd-kernel enforcement, and background subtraction using morphological opening or large median blur with normalization. Pipeline wiring exists in preprocess_pipeline() and is used in main.py. Minor gaps: no explicit tiling/streaming for true 30k memory constraints and background subtraction is on downsampled path only (fine stage uses raw gray).
items.coarse_detection: 5/7 - Provides threshold_for_contours() with morphology cleanup, find_largest_contour() and circularity(), and circle fitting via cv2.minEnclosingCircle plus optional least-squares circle fit. main.py shows scaling back to original coordinates via scale_to_original(). Coarse_detect docstring suggests Hough+fallback strategy, but the provided coarse_detect() body is truncated so full method selection/robustness can’t be fully verified; still, core contour+min-enclosing-circle capability is present.
items.fine_detection: 5/7 - Implements 360/720-ray radial scanning infrastructure (generate_radial_lines, bilinear sample_radial_profile), gradient-based edge detection along profiles (np.gradient with smoothing), and sub-pixel refinement via parabolic interpolation. However, the shown edge detector mainly selects the first significant gradient peak (inner edge) rather than an explicit dual-edge (rising/falling) classifier; and fine_detection.py is truncated mid-function so full dual-edge logic / final circle fit integration can’t be confirmed.
items.statistical_analysis: 4/7 - main.py references robust_circle_fit(), MAD-based sigma_threshold, outlier rejection, and feature classification utilities (classify_feature_points, analyze_angular_coverage), indicating intended robust statistics. But statistical_analysis.py content is not shown, so MAD computation and explicit 3-sigma (or MAD->sigma) rejection cannot be verified in code here; partial credit based on clear integration points and parameters.
items.mask_generation: 4/7 - Mask generation is solid: high-quality circular mask with optional anti-aliasing and an efficient cropped-mask workflow using chord/bounds geometry (compute_chord_crop_bounds, generate_cropped_mask). However, actual chord detection on the circle (low-gradient arc finding) and feature point detection (chord midpoints, high-intensity points, outlier coordinates) are not present in mask_generation.py as shown; feature detection seems delegated to statistical_analysis but that code is not provided, so only cropping/mask parts are confirmed.

eval_report.comment: Good — solid implementation with minor gaps.

coarse_detection.py - Coarse localization for chip ring inner edge detection.

Uses contour-based analysis on a preprocessed (downsampled + thresholded) image
to quickly estimate the circle center and radius.

Approach:
    1. Threshold the preprocessed image (Otsu or adaptive)
    2. Find contours
    3. Select the largest contour (by area) that is roughly circular
    4. Fit a minimum enclosing circle to get initial (cx, cy, r)
"""

fine_detection.py - Fine localization for chip ring inner edge detection.

Refines the coarse circle estimate using:
    1. Radial gradient scanning along many radial lines from the center
    2. Dual-edge detection strategy (gradient magnitude + intensity transition)
    3. Sub-pixel edge localization via parabolic interpolation
    4. Circle fitting on the refined edge points
